question_text: A student investigates the density of different materials. They measure a block of aluminium with a mass of 270 g and a volume of 100 cm³. They then cut the aluminium block into 10 equal pieces. How does the density of each small piece compare to the original block, and why?
correct_answer: The density remains the same (2.7 g/cm³). When matter is divided into smaller pieces, both mass and volume decrease proportionally, so the ratio (density) stays constant because the arrangement and spacing of particles does not change.
explanation: This question requires students to understand that density is an intensive property that does not depend on the amount of material. Although the mass and volume both decrease by a factor of 10, their ratio remains the same because the internal particle structure and spacing remain unchanged.
